Title: Plasma membrane phylloquinone biosynthesis in nonphotosynthetic parasitic plants

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type IV quinone reductase; NAD(P)H oxidoreductase family
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Germinated seeds; likely plasma membrane-associated pool in developing parasitic tissues
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Transmembrane redox signaling and plasma membrane redox homeostasis associated with seed germination, haustorium development, and parasitism
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to support phylloquinone accumulation by catalyzing the penultimate reduction step in the plasma membrane-targeted pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental expression profiling; coexpression network analysis; inference from known QR2 ortholog biochemistry and localization; comparison against poorly expressed PaNDC1
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Well expressed during early development; responsive around germination and haustorium development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Moderate to high during early P. aegyptiaca development
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination; haustorium-inducing factors (HIFs); early pre-host developmental stages
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq from Parasitic Plant Genome Project; coexpression analysis using transcriptome assemblies
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaQR2 was expressed during early P. aegyptiaca development in parallel with phylloquinone-pathway genes. 2. PaQR2, unlike PaNDC1, showed strong coexpression with phylloquinone genes. 3. Its known family properties support replacement of plastidial NDC1 in the penultimate step of plasma membrane phylloquinone biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Phylloquinone pathway module genes; coexpressed with PaMenA2; PaMenG2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The role is strongly supported but not directly proven by enzyme assay in this paper; discussion cites RNAi of QR2 ortholog in Phtheirospermum japonicum reducing haustorium formation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for reducing parasitic haustorium formation or altering plasma membrane redox signaling in parasitic weeds; should be considered together with MenA2/MenG2 due to pathway coupling.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ferric reductase oxidase; flavocytochrome b superfamily
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Participates in transmembrane electron transport potentially linked to phylloquinone-mediated plasma membrane redox function
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as a strongly coexpressed redox partner of phylloquinone genes, suggesting involvement in plasma membrane electron transport during parasitism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression network analysis; developmental expression profiling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highest in prehaustorial and haustorial tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in prehaustorial and haustorial tissues
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaFRO1 was present in the P. aegyptiaca phylloquinone network. 2. Expression peaked in prehaustorial and haustorial tissues. 3. It was proposed to act in plasma membrane electron transport associated with phylloquinone function.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support is associative rather than direct functional validation for metabolite biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target for disrupting parasite redox signaling rather than direct phylloquinone biosynthetic engineering.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification/modulation of ROS in apoplastic redox system associated with phylloquinone pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strong phylloquinone-gene coexpression suggests participation in plasma membrane/apoplastic redox modulation in the holoparasite
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression network analysis; expression profiling; inference from ortholog localization/function
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed during early parasitic development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly coexpressed during early parasitic development
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaCSD1 showed high connectivity with phylloquinone genes. 2. Its ortholog class is known to function extracellularly/apoplastically. 3. It likely contributes to ROS balance in the phylloquinone-linked redox network.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a biosynthetic step gene for phylloquinone itself.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Auxiliary target for weakening parasite oxidative signaling systems.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant control of ROS generated by phylloquinone-linked plasma membrane electron transport
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Very strong coexpression with phylloquinone genes indicates a role in associated plasma membrane redox homeostasis during parasitism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression network analysis; expression profiling; inference from Arabidopsis ortholog localization
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed through early developmental stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly coexpressed through early developmental stages
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaGPX4 had one of the highest phylloquinone-network connectivities. 2. Orthologs are plasma membrane-anchored antioxidants. 3. It likely buffers ROS in the PM phylloquinone-associated redox chain.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support is network-based, not direct biochemical proof.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential secondary target for suppressing parasite PM redox robustness.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MenA/UbiA-type prenyltransferase; integral membrane protein with multiple transmembrane helices
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 1,4-dihydroxy-2-naphthoate and a prenyl diphosphate donor (exact co-substrate unresolved in this system)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Prenylates the naphthoate intermediate in a late step of phylloquinone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct late precursor of phylloquinone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key post-peroxisomal step enabling pathway redirection to plasma membrane
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Germinated seeds; plasma membrane-associated pool in developing parasite tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plasma membrane redox signaling and electron transport associated with parasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides a plasma membrane-localized late biosynthetic step required for noncanonical phylloquinone production in the holoparasite
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PLAS transcript reconstruction; RT-PCR cloning and sequencing; GFP fusion subcellular localization in Nicotiana benthamiana roots; expression profiling; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during P. aegyptiaca development, including early stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during germination and haustorium development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination; haustorium-inducing factors; early developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcript assembly/coexpression; phylogenetic mining of 1KP
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaMenA2 is an N-truncated MenA isoform lacking a plastid transit peptide. 2. PaMenA2 localized to the plasma membrane in transgenic Nicotiana benthamiana roots. 3. The gene supports redirection of the late phylloquinone pathway from plastids to the plasma membrane in P. aegyptiaca.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Co-localized with PM marker AtPIP2A; functionally associated with PaQR2 and PaMenG2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The prenyl co-substrate source in the PM pathway remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Double 35S promoter or 35S promoter for GFP localization constructs in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for gene editing or silencing to disrupt parasite-specific plasma membrane phylloquinone biosynthesis and potentially haustorium-associated signaling.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant of the plasma membrane pathway
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Germinated seeds; plasma membrane-associated pool in developing parasite tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plasma membrane redox signaling and electron transport associated with parasitism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports terminal phylloquinone formation in a plasma membrane-targeted noncanonical pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PLAS transcript reconstruction; RT-PCR confirmation; GFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; E. coli ΔmenG (ubiE) complementation restoring menaquinone production; expression profiling; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during development and coexpressed with phylloquinone genes; PM-targeted MenG2 isoforms are dominantly expressed in related hemiparasites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during germination and haustorium development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed germination; haustorium-inducing factors; early developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcript assembly/coexpression; 1KP mining; bacterial metabolite HPLC complementation readout
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PaMenG2 is an N-truncated MenG isoform lacking plastid targeting. 2. PaMenG2 localized to the plasma membrane in transgenic Nicotiana benthamiana roots. 3. PaMenG2 restored menaquinone biosynthesis in an E. coli menG mutant, confirming canonical methyltransferase activity. 4. The gene is a core terminal enzyme of the plasma membrane phylloquinone pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Co-localized with PM marker AtPIP2A; functionally associated with PaMenA2 and PaQR2
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The complementation directly demonstrates enzymatic competence despite N-terminal truncation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for GFP localization constructs in N. benthamiana; constitutive bacterial expression vector pUCM in E. coli complementation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Top-priority target for disabling parasite plasma membrane phylloquinone production; useful for engineering tests of parasitism dependence on vitamin K-mediated redox processes.
